title: REF 2029
regionWarning: This section is not specific to your country
description: This tab is currently shows compliance with REF2029. You can find CORE Recommendation for REF2029 [here](https://core.ac.uk/ref). We are now working on tools to provide OA compliance checking according to other policies. If you are interested in us helping you to check compliance against a specific policy, please [contact us](https://core.ac.uk/about#contact). While the audit mechanisms haven’t been specified yet, we are currently reviewing the technical specifications in order to provide as much support as possible for the next REF iteration. The current guidance is to continue to follow the REF2024 audit specifications unless otherwise stated.

total.title: Total outputs
total.subTitle: Outputs in the selected timeframe.
total.description: Number of outputs that are within this REF period (from January 1st 2021), for which CORE has data and can calculate compliance. This number is used to create the compliance percentage of a repository.

compliant.title: Compliant
compliant.subTitle: Outputs meeting criteria
compliant.description: Number of outputs that have been deposited inside the 92 day window. The compliance rate is just an estimation of the actual compliance from the point of view of CORE data. UKRI won't use the rate calculated here but it will coordinate with the institutions to check for exceptions and additional isses.

nonCompliant.title: Non-compliant
nonCompliant.subTitle: Outputs not meeting criteria
nonCompliant.description: The number of documents that were deposited outside the 92 day window. Data is calculated from the deposit date that CORE collects from the metadata record and from the publication date, which is a combination from other repository data and Crossref.

unknown.title: Unknown
unknown.subTitle: Outputs need review.
unknown.description: Outputs that are  within this REF period, for which CORE hasn't enough data to calculate compliance.

cross.title: Cross-repository check
cross.subTitle: Deposits in other repositories
cross.description: Non-compliant outputs in your repository for which compliance is met due to deposits in other repositories

chart.title: Deposit time lag
chart.body:
The chart displays outputs' distribution according to deposit time lag.

- **Time lag** is the difference between deposit and publication date
  in the repository.
- **Publication date** is taken from [Crossref](https://www.crossref.org).
  When this is not available, the repository's one used.
- **Deposit date** is calculated using specific techniques.
  On EPrints repositories it is extracted from the *Date Deposited* field,
  while on DSpace the *dc.date.available* field is used.
chart.warning:
Check our  [guidelines](https://core.ac.uk/ref-audit) and enable the
    required support and then notify us once ready, so that we can initiate
    collecting this information from your repository.

noData.body: No deposit time lag data available for the selected time period.

crossRepositoryCheck.title: Cross-repository check
crossRepositoryCheck.description: Non-compliant outputs in your repository for which compliance is met due to deposits in other repositories
crossRepositoryCheck.paymentRequired: Payment required to access this feature

publicationDates.title: Publication dates
publicationDates.tooltip: We compared the publication dates from the repository records with the corresponding publication dates from Crossref. If there is a date mismatch we mark it as “partial” if the dates are close to each other (in the same month), “different” otherwise.
